LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER GOLDEN STATE WARRIORS, LLC'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION. THE COURT MAKES A DISCLOSURE. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. GOLDEN STATE WARRIORS, LLC'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED FOR THE REASONS SET FORTH IN THE ORDER GRANTING THE AUTHORITY'S PETITION TO CONFIRM. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) PETITIONER TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER OAKLAND-ALAMEDA COUNTY COLISEUM AUTHORITY'S PETITION TO CONFIRM FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD AND FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. THE COURT MAKES A DISCLOSURE. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. OAKLAND-ALAMEDA COUNTY COLISEUM AUTHORITY'S PETITION TO CONFIRM FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. PURSUANT TO 39.3.11 OF THE PARTIES' LICENSE AGREEMENT, THE AUTHORITY IS ENTITLED TO RECOVER ITS REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES, DISBURSEMENTS AND COSTS INCURRED IN CONNECTION WITH THIS PETITION AND THE GOLDEN STATE WARRIORS' PETITION TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) PETITIONER TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Final Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Allen, Deputy City Attorney, 510-238-3839 of the Office of the City Attorney for Petitioner.
Daniel Purcell, Esq. 415-391-5400 of Keker Van Nest & Peters for Petitioner.
Joshua Hill, Esq. 415-268-6462 of Morrison Foerster for Respondent Golden State Warriors.
Kent Richland, Esq. of Gaines Martin Stein & Richland LLP for Respondent Golden State Warriors.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court discloses he knows Mr. Richland professionally, however it will not affect the Court's ability to be neutral and impartial. Respondent Argues, provides copies of documents to the Court. Petitioner Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER OAKLAND-ALAMEDA COUNTY COLISEUM AUTHORITY's Petition To Confirm Final Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment.

The Court makes a disclosure. The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Oakland-Alameda County Coliseum Authority's petition to confirm final arbitration award is granted. Pursuant to 39.3.11 of the parties' License Agreement, the Authority is entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees, disbursements and costs incurred in connection with this petition and the Golden State Warriors' petition to vacate the arbitration award. (see word minutes for full details)

(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Petitioner to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Apr-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Allen, Deputy City Attorney, 510-238-3839 of the Office of the City Attorney for Petitioner.
Daniel Purcell, Esq. 415-391-5400 of Keker Van Nest & Peters for Petitioner.
Joshua Hill, Esq. 415-268-6462 of Morrison Foerster for Respondent Golden State Warriors.
Kent Richland, Esq. of Gaines Martin Stein & Richland LLP for Respondent Golden State Warriors.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court discloses he knows Mr. Richland professionally, however it will not affect the Court's ability to be neutral and impartial. Respondent Argues, provides copies of documents to the Court. Petitioner Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER GOLDEN STATE WARRIORS, LLC's PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION.

The Court makes a disclosure. The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Golden State Warriors, LLC's petition to vacate arbitration award is denied for the reasons set forth in the order granting the Authority's petition to confirm. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Petitioner to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
